analysis_2026_07_22: Bloom = threshold(HgcGlow) + gaussian blur + composite(HgcGlowCombineFx). The BLUR sub-component is VERIFIED (PAEGaussianBlur, ddb 16 kernel-faithful). The divergence is in threshold or composite. Both are decoded verbatim in glow.ts: threshold a=clamp((luma709-Threshold)/Softness+0.5,0,1), glow=rgb*a; composite out=(1-a_blurred)*orig + glow_blurred. The faithful note says worst is t=0.1 across ALL params = a whole-bloom-output OFFSET (constant), not a per-param response — suggesting the COMPOSITE (over vs screen/plus) or the threshold luma space differs.
tractable_next_step: Isolate the threshold pass with the transfer oracle (it's pointwise: luma->a->rgb*a) on uniform inputs to VERIFY or characterize HgcGlow independently of the blur+composite. Then isolate the composite. Each is a pointwise/2-input op amenable to the transfer approach. The blur is already verified, so fixing threshold+composite closes Bloom. This is the model for advancing delegated DIVERGED nodes: decompose into sub-computations, isolate each with the transfer/isolation oracle, fix the diverging sub-op.
generalizes: Same decompose-and-isolate applies to Glow (same family), and to the geometry nodes (Earthquake=RNG+displacement, Underwater=multi-octave sinusoid displacement, ZoomBlur=log-polar resample) where the displacement field can be isolated from the resample.
